creator: danfe@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Daniel Engberg from comment #10)
> I think we can close this now since there's a new maintainer who kept using
> tar.xz?
As long as .tar.lz distfile is still smaller, I don't see why shall we close a valid PR.

Michał's "controversy" post header says it is outdated and no longer adequately expresses his attitude towards the two competing formats.  Also, the picture is incomplete without giving a word to the opposite side: https://www.nongnu.org/lzip/xz_inadequate.html.  Debian's (momentarily) decision not to use lzip for compressing *their own packages* is irrelevant to this discussion.

I personally had better experience with .tar.lz and always prefer it; our framework supports it natively  as a first class citizen (USES+=tar:lz) so whenever it gives smaller distfiles, we should switch to it upon next update.
